{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude/skills/session-workflow\n\n# Session Workflow\n\nThis skill expands each step of the core loop: **READ → REVIEW → DECIDE → ACT → VERIFY → DOCUMENT**.\n\n---\n\n## 1. READ — Load Context\n\n- Check if `REPOSITORY.md` exists. If not, invoke the `repo-bootstrap` skill before proceeding.\n- Read `.claude-beat/PRD.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md`\n- If PRD.md references additional files, read those too\n- Read the last 2–3 session logs from `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/`\n- Fetch open issues and PRs via `gh` CLI\n\n---\n\n## 2. REVIEW — Assess Current State\n\nBefore picking new work, check what needs attention right now.\n\n### 2a. Production Health Check (every session)\n\nIf the project has a production or staging environment:\n\n1. Identify the production URL (from memory, `.env`, deploy config, or README). If no production URL can be found, skip the production health check and note \"No production environment detected\" in the session log.\n2. Use `which agent-browser` to locate the browser tool. Always attempt browser verification before falling back to non-visual checks. Open the production URL and verify core functionality works.\n3. Check for visible errors, broken pages, or degraded behavior\n4. If the platform provides logs or monitoring (Render, Vercel, etc.) — check recent error logs\n\n**If production issues are found:**\n- **Critical** (app down, core flow broken, data loss risk): fix immediately in this session, create a PR, deploy\n- **Medium** (non-blocking bugs, UI glitches, non-critical errors): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P1`\n- **Low** (cosmetic, minor warnings): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P2`\n\n### 2b. Follow Up on Previous Sessions\n\nRead the most recent session logs and check for:\n\n- **Deployments**: if something was deployed last session, verify it is live and working (part of 2a)\n- **Human action requests**: if a previous session created a `needs-human` or `blocked` issue, check whether the human has acted on it\n  - If resolved — acknowledge, close the issue, continue with any unblocked work\n  - If stale (no response for 2+ sessions) — add a comment on the issue reminding the owner, and note it in the session log\n- **Open PRs with review comments**: if a PR has unaddressed reviewer feedback, respond and update before opening new PRs\n- **WIP branches**: if the previous session left work-in-progress, pick it up and finish it\n\n### 2c. PRD Sync\n\nScan PRD (and any referenced spec files) for features with no corresponding GitHub issue. For each missing feature:\n\n- Label: `approved` + `prd` + priority (`P0`/`P1`/`P2` matching the PRD)\n- Title prefix: `prd:` (e.g., `prd: add CEFR level selector`)\n- These are pre-approved — no proposal step, no waiting for human reaction\n\nSearch existing issues first to avoid duplicates (`gh issue list --state all`). This step runs every session regardless of other work.\n\n---\n\n## 3. DECIDE — Pick What to Work On\n\nWrite a brief triage summary in your session log before starting work:\n- Production status (healthy / issues found)\n- Follow-ups resolved or escalated\n- What you will work on this session and why\n\nSelect work in this priority order:\n\n1. **Critical production issues** found during REVIEW\n2. **Approved `P0` issues** — launch blockers, critical bugs, critical PRD features\n3. **Open PRs needing attention** — review comments, merge-ready PRs, CI failures\n4. **In-progress work** from previous sessions (WIP branches, draft PRs)\n5. **Approved `P1` and `P2` issues** — P1 first, then P2\n6. **Stale proposals** — if a `proposal` issue has had no human reaction for 2+ sessions, change label to `approved` and comment \"Auto-approving after no objection.\" Never self-approve issues labeled `needs-human` or `blocked`.\n\n---\n\n## 4. ACT — Execute\n\nFor each piece of work:\n1. Implement the change\n2. Run lint, tests, build\n3. Test using `agent-browser` skill if UI-related\n4. Commit and create/update PR (use `pr-lifecycle` skill)\n5. If the primary item finishes quickly and there is a small, self-contained second item, you may take it on. Do not start a third item — leave it for the next session.\n\n---\n\n## 5. VERIFY — Post-Deploy Check\n\nIf you deployed anything this session:\n1. Verify the deployment succeeded\n2. If browser testing tooling is available, open production and confirm the change is live\n3. Check for regressions — test core flows, not just the new feature\n\nIf you opened or updated a PR this session:\n- Verify CI checks are passing before closing the session\n\nIf you did NOT deploy but production had issues in REVIEW:\n- Confirm your fix PR is open and ready for review\n\n---\n\n## 6. DOCUMENT — Close the Session\n\n1. Write session log to `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM.md`\n2. Update `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`\n3. Update `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md` if you learned something new\n4. Ensure no uncommitted changes remain (`git status`)\n5. All branches pushed, all work has a PR (draft is fine for WIP)\n\n---\n\n## Quick Reference: Session Checklist\n\n```\nREAD\n[ ] Context loaded (PRD, memory, logs, issues, PRs)\n\nREVIEW\n[ ] Production health checked\n[ ] Previous session follow-ups addressed\n\nDECIDE\n[ ] Triage summary written\n[ ] Work selected by priority\n\nACT\n[ ] Changes implemented, tested, PR opened\n\nVERIFY\n[ ] CI checks passing (if PR opened)\n[ ] Production check (if deployed)\n\nDOCUMENT\n[ ] Session log written\n[ ] Memory updated\n[ ] No uncommitted changes\n```\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b216bb0e-4934-43a0-8f7a-14f7ea4271cf","uuid":"5f2ffc68-f51e-4efe-a412-8b8623ae3696","timestamp":"2026-03-21T07:00:06.621Z","isSynthetic":true}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019J4U6qAQF1JHkS9snMGLqb","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Product Requirements Document\n     2→\n     3→## Project Name\n     4→\n     5→Bookmate\n     6→\n     7→## Overview\n     8→\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n    10→\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n    12→\n    13→## Goals\n    14→\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n    18→\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n    20→\n    21→### User Authentication\n    22→\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n    24→\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\n    26→\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n    34→\n    35→### Browse Listings\n    36→\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n    38→\n    39→### Join a Listing\n    40→\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n    42→\n    43→### Group Formation\n    44→\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n    49→\n    50→### Notifications\n    51→\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n    55→\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\n    57→\n    58→- Display name\n    59→- Short bio\n    60→\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n    62→\n    63→### Search and Filters\n    64→\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\n    68→\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n    70→\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n    72→\n    73→### Extended User Profile\n    74→\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n    76→- \"Currently reading\" list\n    77→- Favorite genres\n    78→\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n    80→\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n    82→\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n    84→\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\n    86→\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n    88→\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\n    90→\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n    92→\n    93→### Discord Support\n    94→\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n    96→\n    97→## Non-Goals\n    98→\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n   105→\n   106→## Tech Stack\n   107→\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n   113→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b216bb0e-4934-43a0-8f7a-14f7ea4271cf","uuid":"8bf26ca4-c0a1-478d-a8e0-499be05dd3fa","timestamp":"2026-03-21T07:00:10.198Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md","content":"# Product Requirements Document\n\n## Project Name\n\nBookmate\n\n## Overview\n\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n\n## Goals\n\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n\n### User Authentication\n\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n\n### Create a Reading Listing\n\nA user creates a listing with:\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n\n### Browse Listings\n\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n\n### Join a Listing\n\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n\n### Group Formation\n\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n\n### Notifications\n\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n\n### User Profile (Basic)\n\n- Display name\n- Short bio\n\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n\n### Search and Filters\n\n- Search listings by book title or author\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\n\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n\n### Extended User Profile\n\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n- \"Currently reading\" list\n- Favorite genres\n\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n\n### Applicant Approval System\n\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n\n### Rating and Reputation System\n\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n\n### Discord Support\n\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- No payment or monetization features\n- No mobile app (web only)\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n","numLines":113,"startLine":1,"totalLines":113}}}

Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint has warnings (img elements — intentional for Open Library covers)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n- Loading skeletons on all routes (root, listing detail, profile, notifications)\n- Error boundaries on all routes with retry functionality\n- Focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility\n- **Accessibility improvements** (PR #49, merged + deployed):\n  - ARIA labels, roles, expanded states on all interactive elements\n  - Keyboard navigation on BookSearch dropdown (combobox pattern)\n  - autocomplete attributes on auth forms\n  - role=\"alert\" on error messages\n  - Skip-to-content link\n  - role=\"tablist\"/tab/tabpanel on profile tabs\n  - role=\"radiogroup\"/radio on format/platform buttons\n  - role=\"checkbox\" with keyboard support on approval toggle\n\n## Production\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n\n## Open PRs\n- None\n\n## Closed Recently\n- #58: next/image optimization + SEO (robots.txt, sitemap.xml) — merged + deployed\n- #49: Accessibility improvements — ARIA, keyboard nav, screen readers (merged + deployed)\n- #46: Loading skeletons, error boundaries, improved error handling (merged + deployed)\n\n## Open Issues\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting\n- #54: **PAT expires ~March 27** (needs-human) — GH_TOKEN renewal required\n- #57: Test infrastructure (approved, P2)\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Implement **#57 (test infrastructure)** — P2, approved\n2. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~5 days remaining, still needs-human\n3. Check issue #21 for Telegram token response\n4. Check production health\n","numLines":83,"startLine":1,"totalLines":83}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01ANKBaQ43j4GfbdGEQB5vsm","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Learned Patterns\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub Authentication\n     4→- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n     5→  ```bash\n     6→  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n     7→  ```\n     8→- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n     9→- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n    10→\n    11→## Session Flow\n    12→- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n    13→- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n    14→- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n    15→\n    16→## Environment\n    17→- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n    18→- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n    19→- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n    20→- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n    21→- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n    22→- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n    23→\n    24→## Build\n    25→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n    26→- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n    27→- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n    28→- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n    29→- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n    30→\n    31→## Testing\n    32→- Vitest is the test runner: `cd app && npm test`\n    33→- Tests live in `app/__tests__/` with helpers in `app/__tests__/helpers/`\n    34→- Use `vi.hoisted()` with inline `require(\"better-sqlite3\")` to create in-memory DBs for vi.mock factories\n    35→- vi.mock is hoisted above imports — can't reference variables declared after it; use `vi.hoisted()` for shared state\n    36→- Mock bcryptjs to avoid slow hashing (cost factor 12) in unit tests\n    37→- Mock `@/lib/session` to avoid `next/headers` dependency outside request context\n    38→- Route uses `q` param for search (not `search`) and `meeting_format` for format filter (not `format`)\n    39→- ESLint allows `require()` in test files (needed for vi.hoisted pattern)\n    40→- `RequestInit` type needs cast to `NextRequest` for NextRequest constructor compatibility\n    41→\n    42→## Browser Testing\n    43→- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n    44→- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n    45→- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n    46→- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n    47→- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n    48→- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n    49→- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n    50→- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n    51→- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n    52→\n    53→## Deployment\n    54→- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n    55→- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n    56→- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n    57→- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n    58→- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n    59→- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n    60→- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    61→- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    62→\n    63→## React + SQLite\n    64→- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n    65→- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n    66→\n    67→## Security\n    68→- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n    69→- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n    70→- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n    71→- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n    72→- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n    73→\n    74→## Working Directory\n    75→- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n    76→- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n    77→- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n    78→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b216bb0e-4934-43a0-8f7a-14f7ea4271cf","uuid":"82595824-b4c9-485d-9623-1a50cde1f481","timestamp":"2026-03-21T07:00:11.154Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md","content":"# Learned Patterns\n\n## GitHub Authentication\n- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n  ```bash\n  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n  ```\n- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n\n## Session Flow\n- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n\n## Environment\n- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n\n## Build\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n\n## Testing\n- Vitest is the test runner: `cd app && npm test`\n- Tests live in `app/__tests__/` with helpers in `app/__tests__/helpers/`\n- Use `vi.hoisted()` with inline `require(\"better-sqlite3\")` to create in-memory DBs for vi.mock factories\n- vi.mock is hoisted above imports — can't reference variables declared after it; use `vi.hoisted()` for shared state\n- Mock bcryptjs to avoid slow hashing (cost factor 12) in unit tests\n- Mock `@/lib/session` to avoid `next/headers` dependency outside request context\n- Route uses `q` param for search (not `search`) and `meeting_format` for format filter (not `format`)\n- ESLint allows `require()` in test files (needed for vi.hoisted pattern)\n- `RequestInit` type needs cast to `NextRequest` for NextRequest constructor compatibility\n\n## Browser Testing\n- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n\n## Deployment\n- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n\n## React + SQLite\n- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n\n## Security\n- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n\n## Working Directory\n- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n","numLines":78,"startLine":1,"totalLines":78}}}

   34→{\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Base directory for this skill: /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude/skills/session-workflow\\n\\n# Session Workflow\\n\\nThis skill expands each step of the core loop: **READ → REVIEW → DECIDE → ACT → VERIFY → DOCUMENT**.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. READ — Load Context\\n\\n- Check if `REPOSITORY.md` exists. If not, invoke the `repo-bootstrap` skill before proceeding.\\n- Read `.claude-beat/PRD.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md`\\n- If PRD.md references additional files, read those too\\n- Read the last 2–3 session logs from `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/`\\n- Fetch open issues and PRs via `gh` CLI\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. REVIEW — Assess Current State\\n\\nBefore picking new work, check what needs attention right now.\\n\\n### 2a. Production Health Check (every session)\\n\\nIf the project has a production or staging environment:\\n\\n1. Identify the production URL (from memory, `.env`, deploy config, or README). If no production URL can be found, skip the production health check and note \\\"No production environment detected\\\" in the session log.\\n2. Use `which agent-browser` to locate the browser tool. Always attempt browser verification before falling back to non-visual checks. Open the production URL and verify core functionality works.\\n3. Check for visible errors, broken pages, or degraded behavior\\n4. If the platform provides logs or monitoring (Render, Vercel, etc.) — check recent error logs\\n\\n**If production issues are found:**\\n- **Critical** (app down, core flow broken, data loss risk): fix immediately in this session, create a PR, deploy\\n- **Medium** (non-blocking bugs, UI glitches, non-critical errors): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P1`\\n- **Low** (cosmetic, minor warnings): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P2`\\n\\n### 2b. Follow Up on Previous Sessions\\n\\nRead the most recent session logs and check for:\\n\\n- **Deployments**: if something was deployed last session, verify it is live and working (part of 2a)\\n- **Human action requests**: if a previous session created a `needs-human` or `blocked` issue, check whether the human has acted on it\\n  - If resolved — acknowledge, close the issue, continue with any unblocked work\\n  - If stale (no response for 2+ sessions) — add a comment on the issue reminding the owner, and note it in the session log\\n- **Open PRs with review comments**: if a PR has unaddressed reviewer feedback, respond and update before opening new PRs\\n- **WIP branches**: if the previous session left work-in-progress, pick it up and finish it\\n\\n### 2c. PRD Sync\\n\\nScan PRD (and any referenced spec files) for features with no corresponding GitHub issue. For each missing feature:\\n\\n- Label: `approved` + `prd` + priority (`P0`/`P1`/`P2` matching the PRD)\\n- Title prefix: `prd:` (e.g., `prd: add CEFR level selector`)\\n- These are pre-approved — no proposal step, no waiting for human reaction\\n\\nSearch existing issues first to avoid duplicates (`gh issue list --state all`). This step runs every session regardless of other work.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. DECIDE — Pick What to Work On\\n\\nWrite a brief triage summary in your session log before starting work:\\n- Production status (healthy / issues found)\\n- Follow-ups resolved or escalated\\n- What you will work on this session and why\\n\\nSelect work in this priority order:\\n\\n1. **Critical production issues** found during REVIEW\\n2. **Approved `P0` issues** — launch blockers, critical bugs, critical PRD features\\n3. **Open PRs needing attention** — review comments, merge-ready PRs, CI failures\\n4. **In-progress work** from previous sessions (WIP branches, draft PRs)\\n5. **Approved `P1` and `P2` issues** — P1 first, then P2\\n6. **Stale proposals** — if a `proposal` issue has had no human reaction for 2+ sessions, change label to `approved` and comment \\\"Auto-approving after no objection.\\\" Never self-approve issues labeled `needs-human` or `blocked`.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 4. ACT — Execute\\n\\nFor each piece of work:\\n1. Implement the change\\n2. Run lint, tests, build\\n3. Test using `agent-browser` skill if UI-related\\n4. Commit and create/update PR (use `pr-lifecycle` skill)\\n5. If the primary item finishes quickly and there is a small, self-contained second item, you may take it on. Do not start a third item — leave it for the next session.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 5. VERIFY — Post-Deploy Check\\n\\nIf you deployed anything this session:\\n1. Verify the deployment succeeded\\n2. If browser testing tooling is available, open production and confirm the change is live\\n3. Check for regressions — test core flows, not just the new feature\\n\\nIf you opened or updated a PR this session:\\n- Verify CI checks are passing before closing the session\\n\\nIf you did NOT deploy but production had issues in REVIEW:\\n- Confirm your fix PR is open and ready for review\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 6. DOCUMENT — Close the Session\\n\\n1. Write session log to `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM.md`\\n2. Update `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`\\n3. Update `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md` if you learned something new\\n4. Ensure no uncommitted changes remain (`git status`)\\n5. All branches pushed, all work has a PR (draft is fine for WIP)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Quick Reference: Session Checklist\\n\\n```\\nREAD\\n[ ] Context loaded (PRD, memory, logs, issues, PRs)\\n\\nREVIEW\\n[ ] Production health checked\\n[ ] Previous session follow-ups addressed\\n\\nDECIDE\\n[ ] Triage summary written\\n[ ] Work selected by priority\\n\\nACT\\n[ ] Changes implemented, tested, PR opened\\n\\nVERIFY\\n[ ] CI checks passing (if PR opened)\\n[ ] Production check (if deployed)\\n\\nDOCUMENT\\n[ ] Session log written\\n[ ] Memory updated\\n[ ] No uncommitted changes\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"b216bb0e-4934-43a0-8f7a-14f7ea4271cf\",\"uuid\":\"5f2ffc68-f51e-4efe-a412-8b8623ae3696\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-21T07:00:06.621Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n session-workflow\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"b216bb0e-4934-43a0-8f7a-14f7ea4271cf\",\"uuid\":\"f49efa46-c429-4476-aada-d3abe108a739\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-21T07:00:06.622Z\",\"tool_use_result\":{\"success\":true,\"commandName\":\"session-workflow\"}}\n{\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Base directory for this skill: /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude/skills/session-workflow\\n\\n# Session Workflow\\n\\nThis skill expands each step of the core loop: **READ → REVIEW → DECIDE → ACT → VERIFY → DOCUMENT**.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. READ — Load Context\\n\\n- Check if `REPOSITORY.md` exists. If not, invoke the `repo-bootstrap` skill before proceeding.\\n- Read `.claude-beat/PRD.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md`\\n- If PRD.md references additional files, read those too\\n- Read the last 2–3 session logs from `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/`\\n- Fetch open issues and PRs via `gh` CLI\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. REVIEW — Assess Current State\\n\\nBefore picking new work, check what needs attention right now.\\n\\n### 2a. Production Health Check (every session)\\n\\nIf the project has a production or staging environment:\\n\\n1. Identify the production URL (from memory, `.env`, deploy config, or README). If no production URL can be found, skip the production health check and note \\\"No production environment detected\\\" in the session log.\\n2. Use `which agent-browser` to locate the browser tool. Always attempt browser verification before falling back to non-visual checks. Open the production URL and verify core functionality works.\\n3. Check for visible errors, broken pages, or degraded behavior\\n4. If the platform provides logs or monitoring (Render, Vercel, etc.) — check recent error logs\\n\\n**If production issues are found:**\\n- **Critical** (app down, core flow broken, data loss risk): fix immediately in this session, create a PR, deploy\\n- **Medium** (non-blocking bugs, UI glitches, non-critical errors): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P1`\\n- **Low** (cosmetic, minor warnings): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P2`\\n\\n### 2b. Follow Up on Previous Sessions\\n\\nRead the most recent session logs and check for:\\n\\n- **Deployments**: if something was deployed last session, verify it is live and working (part of 2a)\\n- **Human action requests**: if a previous session created a `needs-human` or `blocked` issue, check whether the human has acted on it\\n  - If resolved — acknowledge, close the issue, continue with any unblocked work\\n  - If stale (no response for 2+ sessions) — add a comment on the issue reminding the owner, and note it in the session log\\n- **Open PRs with review comments**: if a PR has unaddressed reviewer feedback, respond and update before opening new PRs\\n- **WIP branches**: if the previous session left work-in-progress, pick it up and finish it\\n\\n### 2c. PRD Sync\\n\\nScan PRD (and any referenced spec files) for features with no corresponding GitHub issue. For each missing feature:\\n\\n- Label: `approved` + `prd` + priority (`P0`/`P1`/`P2` matching the PRD)\\n- Title prefix: `prd:` (e.g., `prd: add CEFR level selector`)\\n- These are pre-approved — no proposal step, no waiting for human reaction\\n\\nSearch existing issues first to avoid duplicates (`gh issue list --state all`). This step runs every session regardless of other work.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. DECIDE — Pick What to Work On\\n\\nWrite a brief triage summary in your session log before starting work:\\n- Production status (healthy / issues found)\\n- Follow-ups resolved or escalated\\n- What you will work on this session and why\\n\\nSelect work in this priority order:\\n\\n1. **Critical production issues** found during REVIEW\\n2. **Approved `P0` issues** — launch blockers, critical bugs, critical PRD features\\n3. **Open PRs needing attention** — review comments, merge-ready PRs, CI failures\\n4. **In-progress work** from previous sessions (WIP branches, draft PRs)\\n5. **Approved `P1` and `P2` issues** — P1 first, then P2\\n6. **Stale proposals** — if a `proposal` issue has had no human reaction for 2+ sessions, change label to `approved` and comment \\\"Auto-approving after no objection.\\\" Never self-approve issues labeled `needs-human` or `blocked`.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 4. ACT — Execute\\n\\nFor each piece of work:\\n1. Implement the change\\n2. Run lint, tests, build\\n3. Test using `agent-browser` skill if UI-related\\n4. Commit and create/update PR (use `pr-lifecycle` skill)\\n5. If the primary item finishes quickly and there is a small, self-contained second item, you may take it on. Do not start a third item — leave it for the next session.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 5. VERIFY — Post-Deploy Check\\n\\nIf you deployed anything this session:\\n1. Verify the deployment succeeded\\n2. If browser testing tooling is available, open production and confirm the change is live\\n3. Check for regressions — test core flows, not just the new feature\\n\\nIf you opened or updated a PR this session:\\n- Verify CI checks are passing before closing the session\\n\\nIf you did NOT deploy but production had issues in REVIEW:\\n- Confirm your fix PR is open and ready for review\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 6. DOCUMENT — Close the Session\\n\\n1. Write session log to `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM.md`\\n2. Update `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`\\n3. Update `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md` if you learned something new\\n4. Ensure no uncommitted changes remain (`git status`)\\n5. All branches pushed, all work has a PR (draft is fine for WIP)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Quick Reference: Session Checklist\\n\\n```\\nREAD\\n[ ] Context loaded (PRD, memory, logs, issues, PRs)\\n\\nREVIEW\\n[ ] Production health checked\\n[ ] Previous session follow-ups addressed\\n\\nDECIDE\\n[ ] Triage summary written\\n[ ] Work selected by priority\\n\\nACT\\n[ ] Changes implemented, tested, PR opened\\n\\nVERIFY\\n[ ] CI checks passing (if PR opened)\\n[ ] Production check (if deployed)\\n\\nDOCUMENT\\n[ ] Session log written\\n[ ] Memory updated\\n[ ] No uncommitted changes\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"b216bb0e-4934-43a0-8f7a-14f7ea4271cf\",\"uuid\":\"5f2ffc68-f51e-4efe-a412-8b8623ae3696\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-21T07:00:06.621Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"message_stop\"},\"session_id\":\"b216bb0e-4934-43a0-8f7a-14f7ea4271cf\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"8680119e-4a35-4834-8dd2-a0e836636fb1\"}\n{\"type\":\"rate_limit_event\",\"rate_limit_info\":{\"status\":\"allowed\",\"resetsAt\":1774080000,\"rateLimitType\":\"five_hour\",\"overageStatus\":\"rejected\",\"overageDisabledReason\":\"out_of_credits\",\"isUsingOverage\":false},\"uuid\":\"23837f17-1e5b-4a97-a40f-cdbf21f9cd51\",\"session_id\":\"b216bb0e-4934-43a0-8f7a-14f7ea4271cf\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"message_start\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-opus-4-6\",\"id\":\"msg_01GEDDT5RGAL5oBT4vcx6MVm\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":2,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":1621,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":13766,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":1621},\"output_tokens\":9,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}}},\"session_id\":\"b216bb0e-4934-43a0-8f7a-14f7ea4271cf\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"f0278347-80be-4be3-8bef-aad741010c8c\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_start\",\"index\":0,\"content_block\":{\"type\":\"thinking\",\"thinking\":\"\",\"signature\":\"\"}},\"session_id\":\"b216bb0e-4934-43a0-8f7a-14f7ea4271cf\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"d30bd55d-0ddf-415b-9a8f-a774dba8fa01\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_delta\",\"index\":0,\"delta\":{\"type\":\"thinking_delta\",\"thinking\":\"Let\"}},\"session_id\":\"b216bb0e-4934-43a0-8f7a-14f7ea4271cf\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"6e90a579-e07b-40cd-9898-f88b2982 Summary\n     4→- **Production**: Healthy (uptime 3h, 4 users, 7 open listings, PM2 online)\n     5→- **#54 PAT expiry**: Still needs-human, ~6 days remaining\n     6→- **#21 Telegram token**: Still {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01KfTtcvA2nRcxUz9nWzT3Ar","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_018FfPDgwR6ufcYh2UeSL2yo","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2) && gh issue list --state all --limit 100 --json number,title,state,labels 2>/dev/null | python3 -c \"\nimport json, sys\nissues = json.load(sys.stdin)\nfor i in issues:\n    labels = ', '.join(l['name'] for l in i.get('labels', []))\n    print(f\\\"#{i['number']} [{i['state']}] {i['title']} ({labels})\\\")\" 2>/dev/null","description":"List all issues with labels"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1057,"cache_read_input_tokens":36970,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1057},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b216bb0e-4934-43a0-8f7a-14f7ea4271cf","uuid":"00a639ee-8668-484d-bdc1-82803f8a9781"}

Currently reading: groups that are full (active) or start_date <= today\n    53→  const currentlyReading = readings.filter(\n    54→    (r) => r.is_full === 1 || r.start_date <= today\n    55→  );\n    56→\n    57→  // Reading history: completed groups (full + start_date in the past)\n    58→  // Upcoming: not yet started and not full\n    59→  const history = readings.filter(\n    60→    (r) => const active = readings.filter(\n    68→    (r) => !history.includes(r) && !upcoming.includes(r) && (r.is_full === 1 || r.start_date <= today)\n    69→  );\n    70→\n    71→  return 78→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b216bb0e-4934-43a0-8f7a-14f7ea4271cf","uuid":"10c6d969-d6d8-44c5-a831-6adf6b7f6993","timestamp":"2026-03-21T11:53:22.429Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/reading/route.ts","content":"    JOIN users u ON u.id = l.author_id\n    WHERE lm.user_id = ?\n    ORDER BY lm.joined_at DESC\n  `).all(session.userId) as ReadingRow[];\n\n  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n\n  // Currently reading: groups that are full (active) or start_date <= today\n  const currentlyReading = readings.filter(\n    (r) => r.is_full === 1 || r.start_date <= today\n  );\n\n  // Reading history: completed groups (full + start_date in the past)\n  // Upcoming: not yet started and not full\n  const history = readings.filter(\n    (r) => r.is_full === 1 && r.start_date < today\n  );\n\n  const upcoming = readings.filter(\n    (r) => r.is_full === 0 && r.start_date > today\n  );\n\n  const active = readings.filter(\n    (r) => !history.includes(r) && !upcoming.includes(r) && (r.is_full === 1 || r.start_date <= today)\n  );\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    active,\n    history,\n    upcoming,\n    total: readings.length,\n  });\n}\n","numLines":34,"startLine":45,"totalLines":78}}}

successfully."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b216bb0e-4934-43a0-8f7a-14f7ea4271cf","uuid":"bfd8a660-ed1e-4c1f-b0a1-019b97fc86ff","timestamp":"2026-03-21T11:53:26.373Z","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/reading/route.ts","oldString":"  // Currently reading: groups that are full (active) or start_date <= today\n  const currentlyReading = readings.filter(\n    (r) => r.is_full === 1 || r.start_date <= today\n  );\n\n  // Reading history: completed groups (full + start_date in the past)\n  // Upcoming: not yet started and not full","newString":"  // Reading history: completed groups (full + start_date in the past)\n  // Upcoming: not yet started and not full","originalFile":"import { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n\ninterface ReadingRow {\n  id: number;\n  book_title: string;\n  book_author: string;\n  book_cover_url: string;\n  reading_pace: string;\n  start_date: string;\n  meeting_format: string;\n  is_full: number;\n  telegram_link: string;\n  member_count: number;\n  max_group_size: number;\n  author_name: string;\n  joined_at: string;\n}\n\nexport async function GET() {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  // Get all listings the user is a member of (including ones they authored)\n  const readings = db.prepare(`\n    SELECT\n      l.id,\n      l.book_title,\n      l.book_author,\n      l.book_cover_url,\n      l.reading_pace,\n      l.start_date,\n      l.meeting_format,\n      l.is_full,\n      l.telegram_link,\n      l.max_group_size,\n      u.display_name as author_name,\n      lm.joined_at,\n      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n    FROM listing_members lm\n    JOIN listings l ON l.id = lm.listing_id\n    JOIN users u ON u.id = l.author_id\n    WHERE lm.user_id = ?\n    ORDER BY lm.joined_at DESC\n  `).all(session.userId) as ReadingRow[];\n\n  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n\n  // Currently reading: groups that are full (active) or start_date <= today\n  const currentlyReading = readings.filter(\n    (r) => r.is_full === 1 || r.start_date <= today\n  );\n\n  // Reading history: completed groups (full + start_date in the past)\n  // Upcoming: not yet started and not full\n  const history = readings.filter(\n    (r) => r.is_full === 1 && r.start_date < today\n  );\n\n  const upcoming = readings.filter(\n    (r) => r.is_full === 0 && r.start_date > today\n  );\n\n  const active = readings.filter(\n    (r) => !history.includes(r) && !upcoming.includes(r) && (r.is_full === 1 || r.start_date <= today)\n  );\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    active,\n    history,\n    upcoming,\n    total: readings.length,\n  });\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":49,"oldLines":11,"newStart":49,"newLines":6,"lines":[" ","   const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];"," ","-  // Currently reading: groups that are full (active) or start_date <= today","-  const currentlyReading = readings.filter(","-    (r) => r.is_full === 1 || r.start_date <= today","-  );","-","   // Reading history: completed groups (full + start_date in the past)","   // Upcoming: not yet started and not full","   const history = readings.filter("]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_013uaocB5zUfewpCJY5zDYJh","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01BB9QrgHjFbycWaSfRvxrHT","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2) && gh issue view 54 --json body,comments 2>/dev/null | python3 -c \"\nimport json, sys\nd = json.load(sys.stdin)\nprint('Body:', d.get('body','')[:300])\nprint()\nprint(f'Comments: {len(d.get(\\\"comments\\\", []))}')\nfor c in d.get('comments', []):\n    print(f\\\"--- {c['author']['login']} at {c['createdAt']}:\\\")\n    print(c['body'][:200])\n    print()\n\"","description":"View PAT issue details"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":255,"cache_read_input_tokens":48432,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":255},"output_tokens":25,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b216bb0e-4934-43a0-8f7a-14f7ea4271cf","uuid":"e3e7b6b1-99f7-49c5-97f7-9af02573c805"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01BB9QrgHjFbycWaSfRvxrHT","type":"tool_result","content":"Body: ## What's needed\n\nThe GitHub personal access token (classic) **\"test-instance\"** used by the agent will **expire in ~7 days** (around March 27, 2026).\n\n## Why\n\nWithout a valid PAT, the agent cannot:\n- Push commits to the repository\n- Create/merge pull requests\n- Manage issues and project board\n- Run\n\nComments: 0","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b216bb0e-4934-43a0-8f7a-14f7ea4271cf","uuid":"7c4fb1d0-2ebf-4b96-b7a6-4d611d5582c4","timestamp":"2026-03-21T11:53:46.505Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"Body: ## What's needed\n\nThe GitHub personal access token (classic) **\"test-instance\"** used by the agent will **expire in ~7 days** (around March 27, 2026).\n\n## Why\n\nWithout a valid PAT, the agent cannot:\n- Push commits to the repository\n- Create/merge pull requests\n- Manage issues and project board\n- Run\n\nComments: 0","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019YZhJfNxnHEEcbouhZmSAy","type":"tool_result","content":"<persisted-output>\nOutput too large (175.4KB).     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \\\"next/server\\\";\\n     2→import db from \\\"@/lib/db\\\";\\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \\\"@/lib/session\\\";\\n     4→\\n     5→/**\\n     6→ * GET /api/profile/reputation?userId=X\\n     7→ *\\n     8→ * Returns a user's reputation score and rating breakdown.\\n     9→ * If no userId is provided, returns the current user's reputation.\\n    10→ */\\n    11→export async function GET(req: NextRequest) {\\n    12→  const session = await requireAuth();\\n    13→  if (!session) {\\n    14→    return NextResponse.json(\\n    15→      { error: \\\"Authentication required\\\" },\\n    16→      { status: 401 }\\n    17→    );\\n    18→  }\\n    19→\\n    20→  const userIdParam = req.nextUrl.searchParams.get(\\\"userId\\\");\\n    21→  const userId = userIdParam ? parseInt(userIdParam, 10) : session.userId;\\n    22→  const isOwnProfile = userId === session.userId;\\n    23→\\n    24→  if (userIdParam && isNaN(userId as number)) {\\n    25→    return NextResponse.json(\\n    26→      { error: \\\"Invalid userId parameter\\\" },\\n    27→      { status: 400 }\\n    28→    );\\n    29→  }\\n    30→\\n    31→  // Average score\\n    32→  const stats = db\\n    33→    .prepare(\\n    34→      `SELECT\\n    35→        COUNT(*) as total_ratings,\\n    36→        ROUND(AVG(score), 1) as average_score,\\n    37→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as five_star,\\n    38→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 4 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as four_star,\\n    39→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as three_star,\\n    40→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as two_star,\\n    41→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as one_star\\n    42→       FROM ratings\\n    43→       WHERE rated_user_id = ?`\\n    44→    )\\n    45→    .get(userId) as {\\n    46→      total_ratings: number;\\n    47→      average_score: number | null;\\n    48→      five_star: number;\\n    49→      four_star: number;\\n    50→      three_star: number;\\n    51→      two_star: number;\\n    52→      one_star: number;\\n    53→    };\\n    54→\\n    55→  // Number of completed groups\\n    56→  const completedGroups = db\\n    57→    .prepare(\\n    58→      `SELECT COUNT(*) as count\\n    59→       FROM listing_members lm\\n    60→       JOIN listings l ON lm.listing_id = l.id\\n    61→       WHERE lm.user_id = ? AND l.is_full = 1`\\n    62→    )\\n    63→    .get(userId) as { count: number };\\n    64→\\n    65→  // Number of groups where this user has given ratings\\n    66→  const groupsRated = db\\n    67→    .prepare(\\n    68→      `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT listing_id) as count\\n    69→       FROM ratings\\n    70→       WHERE rater_id = ?`\\n    71→    )\\n    72→    .get(userId) as { count: number };\\n    73→\\n    74→  // Recent ratings received (with anonymized feedback)\\n    75→  const recentRatings = db\\n    76→    .prepare(\\n    77→      `SELECT r.score, r.comment, r.created_at, l.book_title\\n    78→       FROM ratings r\\n    79→       JOIN listings l ON r.listing_id = l.id\\n    80→       WHERE r.rated_user_id = ?\\n    81→       ORDER BY r.created_at DESC\\n    82→       LIMIT 10`\\n    83→    )\\n    84→    .all(userId) as {\\n    85→      score: number;\\n    86→      comment: string;\\n    87→      created_at: string;\\n    88→      book_title: string;\\n    89→    }[];\\n    90→\\n    91→  // Strip comments from ratings when viewing other users' profiles (privacy)\\n    92→  const sanitizedRatings = isOwnProfile\\n    93→    ? recentRatings\\n    94→    : recentRatings.map(({ score, created_at, book_title }) => ({\\n    95→        score,\\n    96→        created_at,\\n    97→        book_title,\\n    98→      }));\\n    99→\\n   100→  return NextResponse.json({\\n   101→    reputation: {\\n   102→      averageScore: stats.average_score || 0,\\n   103→      totalRatings: stats.total_ratings,\\n   104→      completedGroups: completedGroups.count,\\n   105→      groupsRated: groupsRated.count,\\n   106→      breakdown: {\\n   107→        5: stats.five_star || 0,\\n   108→        4: stats.four_star || 0,\\n   109→        3: stats.three_star || 0,\\n   110→        2: stats.two_star || 0,\\n   111→        1: stats.one_star || 0,\\n   112→      },\\n   113→      recentRatings: sanitizedRatings,\\n   114→    },\\n   115→  });\\n   116→}\\n   117→\"}]},\"uuid\":\"fc69e751-8937-4f70-900d-37e5c446c23a\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-21T11:53:54.489Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"6eb0ff82-a245-47da-9e9e-12ae47de9bee\",\"userType\":\"external\",\"entrypoint\":\"sdk-cli\",\"cwd\":\"/root/Projects/bookmate/app\",\"sessionId\":\"b216bb0e-4934-43a0-8f7a-14f7ea4271cf\",\"version\":\"2.1.78\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"slug\":\"crispy-sparking-shell\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"fc69e751-8937-4f70-900d-37e5c446c23a\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"agentId\":\"af8916a0d1621f3e4\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-4-6\",\"id\":\"msg_01FxMwYddoXHKiR89Y3ii8b6\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_016h8vrqyuCWC9UBGeDWj8BG\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/telegram/route.ts\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":1,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":3116,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":22238,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":3116,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":0},\"output_tokens\":25,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CZGBQ1bsqih9Bhmw8mGP2\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"4738d6d7-6e40-4761-ba06-9b5ca1c3f8f7\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-21T11:53:57.835Z\",\"userType\":\"external\",\"entrypoint\":\"sdk-cli\",\"cwd\":\"/root/Projects/bookmate/app\",\"sessionId\":\"b216bb0e-4934-43a0-8f7a-14f7ea4271cf\",\"version\":\"2.1.78\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"slug\":\"crispy-sparking-shell\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"4738d6d7-6e40-4761-ba06-9b5ca1c3f8f7\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"agentId\":\"af8916a0d1621f3e4\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_016h8vrqyuCWC9UBGeDWj8BG\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_016h8vrqyuCWC9UBGeDWj8BG\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-21T11:53:57.839Z\",\"u     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \\\"next/server\\\";\\n     2→import db from \\\"@/lib/db\\\";\\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \\\"@/lib/session\\\";\\n     4→import { one-click group creation.\\n    10→ * Only available to the listing author when the group is full\\n    11→ * and no Telegram link has been set yet.\\n    12→ */\\n    13→export async function GET(\\n    14→  _req: NextRequest,\\n    15→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\\n    16→) {\\n    17→  const session = await requireAuth();\\n    18→  if (!session) {\\n    19→    return NextResponse.json(\\n    20→      { error: \\\"Authentication required\\\" },\\n    21→      { status: 401 }\\n    22→    );\\n    23→  }\\n    24→\\n    25→  if (!isBotConfigured()) {\\n    26→    return NextResponse.json(\\n    27→      { error: \\\"Telegram bot not configured\\\", botConfigured: false },\\n    28→      { status: 404 }\\n    29→    );\\n    30→  }\\n    31→\\n    32→  const { id } = await params;\\n    33→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\\n    34→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\\n    35→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \\\"Invalid listing ID\\\" }, { status: 400 });\\n    36→  }\\n    37→\\n    38→  const listing = db\\n    39→    .prepare(\\\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\\\")\\n    40→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\\n    41→\\n    42→  if (!listing) {\\n    43→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \\\"Listing not found\\\" }, { status: 404 });\\n    44→  }\\n    45→\\n    46→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\\n    47→    return NextResponse.json(\\n    48→      { error: \\\"Only the organizer can create the Telegram group\\\" },\\n    49→      { status: 403 }\\n    50→    );\\n    51→  }\\n    52→\\n    53→  if (!listing.is_full) {\\n    54→    return NextResponse.json(\\n    55→      { error: \\\"Group must be full before creating a Telegram group\\\" },\\n    56→      { status: 400 }\\n    57→    );\\n    58→  }\\n    59→\\n    60→  if (listing.telegram_link) {\\n    61→    return NextResponse.json(\\n    62→      { error: \\\"Telegram link already set\\\", telegramLink: listing.telegram_link },\\n    63→      { status: 400 }\\n    64→    );\\n    65→  }\\n    66→\\n    67→  const deepLink = await generateGroupDeepLink(listingId);\\n    68→  if (!deepLink) {\\n    69→    return NextResponse.json(\\n    70→      { error: \\\"Failed to generate deep link. Check bot configuration.\\\" },\\n    71→      { status: 500 }\\n    72→    );\\n    73→  }\\n    74→\\n    75→  return NextResponse.json({\\n    76→    deepLink,\\n    77→    instructions:\\n    78→      \\\"Click the link to open Telegram and create a group. (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\\n    30→    return NextResponse.json(\\n    31→      { error: \\\"You cannot apply to your own listing\\\" },\\n    32→      { status: 400 }\\n    33→ (existingApp.status === \\\"pending\\\") {\\n    65→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \\\"You have already applied\\\" }, { status: 400 });\\n    66→    }\\n    67→    if (existingApp.status ===
